data.title: GSK Discloses July 22 Executive ADS Acquisition by U.S. President
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

GlaxoSmithKline ( (GSK) ) has issued an announcement.

On July 22, 2026, GSK notified the market of a personal dealing transaction by Maya Martinez-Davis, its President, US, involving the acquisition of notional American Depositary Shares through her Executive Supplemental Savings Plan account. The transaction, executed on the New York Stock Exchange at a price of $50.76 per ADS for a volume of 9.407 ADS, reflects standard participation by a senior U.S. executive in the company’s long-term savings and incentive arrangements.

The disclosure, formally filed with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission on July 24, 2026, underscores GSK’s adherence to regulatory requirements governing transactions by persons discharging managerial responsibilities. While the scale of the acquisition is modest, such filings provide transparency for investors around executive-linked holdings in GSK’s U.S.-traded securities and reinforce governance practices expected of a major global biopharma issuer.

The most recent analyst rating on (GSK) stock is a Hold
with a $70.00 price target.
To see the full list of analyst forecasts on GlaxoSmithKline stock,
see the GSK Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on GSK Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, GSK is a Neutral.

The score is driven primarily by solid financial performance (strong margins and cash generation, though with leverage and variability) and supportive valuation (low P/E with a ~3.5% dividend). This is meaningfully offset by weak technicals (below key moving averages with negative MACD), while the latest earnings call was broadly constructive on guidance and specialty-led momentum but highlighted H2 phasing and launch/access headwinds.

To see Spark’s full report on GSK stock,
click here.

More about GlaxoSmithKline

GSK plc is a global biopharma company focused on uniting science, technology and talent to get ahead of disease. The group develops and markets prescription medicines and vaccines across major therapeutic areas, with a strong presence in the U.S. market and listings that include American Depositary Shares on the New York Stock Exchange.

Headquartered in London and registered in England and Wales, GSK operates as one of the leading international pharmaceutical and biopharmaceutical issuers. Its securities reporting obligations include regular filings with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission as a foreign private issuer under Form 20-F rules.

Average Trading Volume: 3,981,904

Technical Sentiment Signal: Buy

Current Market Cap: $101.9B

data.title: Digital Brands Group Secures New Financing and Equity Line
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

Digital Brands Group ( (DBGI) ) has issued an update.

On July 23, 2026, Digital Brands Group, Inc. entered into a securities purchase agreement to issue an unsecured convertible promissory note with a principal amount of $3,529,412, tied to a $3,000,000 subscription, to fund general working capital and repay certain liabilities. The note, which matures on January 23, 2027, requires staged repayments from October 2026 through January 2027, carries a 20% default interest rate, and allows default-triggered conversion into common stock subject to Nasdaq ownership caps and beneficial ownership limits.

The company also executed an equity line of credit on July 23, 2026, giving it the right to sell up to $100,000,000 of common stock to the same purchaser over a period ending as late as July 23, 2029, with pricing tied to discounted market trading levels and strict caps on ownership and issuance. In parallel, Digital Brands signed a placement agent agreement with Aegis Capital Corp. and a registration rights agreement requiring prompt registration of the securities for resale, underscoring a strategy to bolster liquidity while shifting some risk to shareholders through potential dilution and exposure to market-driven pricing.

Spark’s Take on DBGI Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, DBGI is a Neutral.

The score is primarily held down by very weak financial performance (sharp margin collapse, large losses, and deeply negative operating/free cash flow) and bearish technical conditions (below major moving averages with negative MACD and low RSI/Stoch). Corporate events provide some upside catalysts (guidance/partnerships, dilution overhang reduction, insider buying), but they do not offset the current fundamental and trend weakness; valuation signals are limited given a negative P/E and no dividend yield provided.

To see Spark’s full report on DBGI stock,
click here.

More about Digital Brands Group

Digital Brands Group, Inc. operates in the consumer and retail sector, focusing on branded apparel and related consumer products distributed through digital and other channels. The company targets growth via capital markets financing to support working capital needs and balance sheet management, reflecting an emphasis on maintaining liquidity and operational flexibility in a competitive retail environment.

By entering structured financing arrangements, Digital Brands positions itself to access additional equity capital over time, albeit with potential dilution constraints tied to stockholder approvals and exchange rules.

Average Trading Volume: 5,346,445

Technical Sentiment Signal: Sell

Current Market Cap: $11.38M

data.title: Lisata Therapeutics Ends Kuva Merger, Starts Strategic Review
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

Lisata Therapeutics ( (LSTA) ) has shared an announcement.

On July 24, 2026, Lisata Therapeutics terminated its March 6, 2026 merger agreement with Kuva Labs and Kuva Acquisition Corp. after the buyer failed to accept and pay for tendered shares when the offer expired on July 20, 2026. Kuva Labs informed Lisata it could not secure sufficient financing, prompting the return of all tendered shares and triggering a $2 million termination fee owed to Lisata.

Lisata has reserved rights to pursue further legal remedies, but noted its limited financial resources and the buyer’s potential inability to satisfy any judgment may constrain recoveries. In response to the failed transaction, the board plans a strategic review to enhance shareholder value, considering options such as a reverse merger, other combinations, asset sales or dissolution, with no set timetable and no assurance any deal will be completed.

The most recent analyst rating on (LSTA) stock is a Buy
with a $15.00 price target.

see the LSTA Stock Forecast page.

The score is held down primarily by very weak financial performance (minimal revenue, large losses, and sustained cash burn despite no debt) and bearish technicals (price well below major moving averages with negative MACD). Valuation is only mildly supportive given a negative P/E and no dividend, while recent merger-related events introduce additional downside uncertainty due to financing and timing risks.

On 24 July 2026, GSK plc reported that 70,759 additional ordinary shares of 31¼ pence each were admitted to trading on the London Stock Exchange’s Main Market under its existing block listing. The new shares, issued in connection with the GlaxoSmithKline plc Share Save Plan 2022, are fully fungible with the company’s existing stock, bringing the total number of ordinary shares in issue and admitted to trading to 4,316,292,310.

An update from Provident Financial Holdings ( (PROV) ) is now available.

Provident Financial Holdings, Inc., the parent of Provident Savings Bank, F.S.B., announced that President and Chief Executive Officer Donavon P. Ternes will join the Boards of Directors of both the corporation and the bank beginning July 23, 2026, filling vacancies created by the death of Director William E. Thomas in April 2026. Ternes, who has served as CEO since January 2024 and has held progressively senior roles since joining in 2000, will serve on the boards until the corporation’s 2027 and the bank’s 2026 annual stockholder meetings.

The bank also appointed Michael S. Van Stockum, age 63, as Senior Vice President and Chief Lending Officer effective July 23, 2026, reflecting a consolidation of experienced leadership in its lending operations. Van Stockum, who has more than 40 years of lending and credit management experience and has been with the bank since 2007 in roles including Commercial Real Estate Loan Administrator and Community Reinvestment Act Officer, has no reportable related-party transactions or family relationships with current directors or executives, underscoring governance and compliance considerations for stakeholders.

The most recent analyst rating on (PROV) stock is a Hold
with a $17.00 price target.

data.title: Profound Medical Highlights New TULSA Quality-of-Life Data
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

Profound Medical ( (TSE:PRN) ) has provided an update.

On July 24, 2026, Profound Medical reported new data from the Level 1 post-market CAPTAIN randomized controlled trial comparing its TULSA Procedure with robotic radical prostatectomy in men with organ-confined, intermediate-risk prostate cancer. At one month, patients treated with whole-gland TULSA saw no median change in penile length, versus a 0.65 cm median reduction after robotic radical prostatectomy, with a statistically significant 0.42 cm between-arm difference.

The findings, presented by UT Southwestern’s Xiaosong Meng at the Society of Robotic Surgery Annual Meeting, add quality-of-life context to earlier CAPTAIN results showing better preservation of erectile function and urinary continence at six months for TULSA patients. The new data support Profound’s strategy to position TULSA as a less invasive alternative to surgical prostatectomy, potentially strengthening its competitive footing in prostate cancer treatment and underscoring benefits that may resonate with patients and clinicians focused on functional outcomes.

The most recent analyst rating on (TSE:PRN) stock is a Buy
with a C$12.00 price target.
To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Profound Medical stock,
see the TSE:PRN Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on PRN Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, PRN is a Neutral.

The score is held back primarily by weak financial performance—very large losses and sustained negative free cash flow despite strong revenue growth and high gross margins. Technicals are a meaningful offset, with the stock in an uptrend and positive momentum signals, though near-overbought readings reduce conviction. Valuation remains constrained by negative earnings and the absence of dividend support.

To see Spark’s full report on PRN stock,
click here.

More about Profound Medical

Profound Medical Corp. is a commercial-stage medical device company specializing in interventional MRI (iMRI) procedures for minimally invasive, incision-free therapies. Its flagship TULSA-PRO platform enables MRI-guided prostate tissue ablation, while its Sonalleve system offers non-surgical, MRI-guided treatments for bone metastases, desmoid tumors, osteoid osteoma, and gynecologic conditions such as uterine fibroids and adenomyosis.

The company’s technologies are cleared or approved in major global markets including the U.S., Europe, Canada, China, Saudi Arabia, India, Australia/New Zealand, and the UAE, positioning Profound as a growing player in image-guided, personalized cancer and gynecologic care. By focusing on organ-sparing approaches that aim to reduce side effects like urinary incontinence and erectile dysfunction, Profound targets patients seeking alternatives to traditional surgery or radiation.

Average Trading Volume: 11,183

Technical Sentiment Signal: Buy

Current Market Cap: C$358.3M

Learn more about PRN stock on TipRanks’ Stock Analysis page.

Gorman-Rupp Company ( (GRC) ) just unveiled an announcement.

On July 23, 2026, Gorman-Rupp announced that Executive Vice President and Chief Financial Officer James C. Kerr plans to retire, having served as CFO since 2017 after joining the company in 2016. Kerr, credited with elevating the firm’s finance, IT, strategic planning, investor relations, and acquisition capabilities, will step down as CFO on October 1, 2026, then remain as a senior advisor until a later retirement date.

The company named current Vice President of Finance, 41-year-old Ronald F. Stoops, as Kerr’s successor effective October 1, 2026, formalizing the transition in a July 24, 2026 press release. Stoops, who joined Gorman-Rupp in 2020 and has led finance since January 2025, brings experience in financial reporting, tax compliance, strategic planning, and prior roles at a global manufacturer and KPMG, with the company signaling a continuity-focused succession free of related-party or conflict-of-interest concerns.

Sidus Space, a space and defense technology company focused on dual-use satellite manufacturing, AI-driven space-based data solutions, mission planning services, and space and defense hardware, operates from Florida’s Space Coast with a 35,000-square-foot integration and testing facility. data.title: Verizon Extends CEO Daniel Schulman’s Contract to 2028
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

Verizon ( (VZ) ) has shared an update.

On July 23, 2026, Verizon Communications Inc. amended the employment agreement of Chief Executive Officer Daniel H. Schulman, extending the initial term of his contract by one year to December 31, 2028, with automatic one-year renewals thereafter unless either party provides at least 90 days’ notice. For calendar year 2028, Schulman will receive no less than his 2027 base salary and target annual incentive, plus a sole long‑term incentive award with a target value of at least $25 million granted in early 2027 on terms aligned with other top executives, with vesting fully accelerated if his employment ends due to a defined succession event.

The amendment also provides that Schulman’s compensation beyond 2028 will be set by the board in line with decisions for Verizon’s most senior executives, underscoring the board’s intent to retain flexibility over long‑term pay while signaling confidence in his leadership through 2028. The clarified structure of salary, incentives, and succession‑related vesting protections may influence investor perception of Verizon’s leadership stability and succession planning, while highlighting the company’s continued reliance on high-value, performance‑linked pay to align executive interests with long‑term corporate goals.

The most recent analyst rating on (VZ) stock is a Hold
with a $46.00 price target.
To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Verizon stock,
see the VZ Stock Forecast page.

data.title: Surgery Partners Announces Idaho Falls Hospital Divestiture Agreement
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

Surgery Partners ( (SGRY) ) just unveiled an announcement.

On July 21, 2026, Surgery Partners and Intermountain Health placed into escrow signed agreements under which Intermountain would acquire Surgery Partners’ ownership interests in Mountain View Hospital and Idaho Falls Community Hospital, valuing the Idaho Falls facilities at about $1.15 billion and providing approximately $795 million in consideration to Surgery Partners, subject to customary adjustments. The transaction, announced publicly on July 24, 2026, marks a major step in Surgery Partners’ portfolio optimization strategy, is contingent on physician approvals, regulatory clearances and other closing conditions, preserves existing physician ownership of Mountain View Hospital, and supports the company’s strategic shift toward ambulatory surgery centers while it reaffirms its 2026 revenue and Adjusted EBITDA outlook excluding the deal’s impact.

The sale involves hospital operations in Idaho Falls that include 126 beds, more than 150 physicians and a broad mix of surgical and acute care services, with Intermountain positioned as the new owner alongside longstanding physician partners. Company leadership framed the divestiture as simplifying ongoing operations, sharpening strategic focus and potentially enhancing long-term shareholder value, while warning that completion is not assured until all approvals are secured and noting that final net cash proceeds cannot yet be reasonably estimated.

The most recent analyst rating on (SGRY) stock is a Buy
with a $19.00 price target.
To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Surgery Partners stock,
see the SGRY Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on SGRY Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, SGRY is a Neutral.

Overall score reflects solid cash generation and reiterated guidance, offset by high leverage and ongoing net losses. Technical signals are neutral-to-soft, and valuation is constrained by negative earnings and no dividend yield.

To see Spark’s full report on SGRY stock,
click here.

LFTD Partners ( (LIFD) ) has shared an announcement.

On July 22, 2026, Lifted Made agreed to sell its 11,238-square-foot principal operations building at 5511 95th Avenue in Kenosha, Wisconsin, to Ad Real Estate Group for $1.5 million, after having bought the property in December 2023 and carrying a mortgage balance of $852,755 at year-end 2025. The transaction, which includes $150,000 in earnest money and is scheduled to close around September 16, 2026 subject to customary real estate contingencies, will see Lifted convey clear title and prorate taxes while using net proceeds to repay the mortgage and consolidate operations into other leased Kenosha facilities.

The Agreement was executed on July 22, 2026 by company vice chairman and COO Nicholas S. Warrender and is structured without a financing contingency for the buyer. Management’s plan to shift out of ownership of its main plant and into leased space marks a move to streamline Lifted’s real estate footprint and reduce debt, potentially improving balance sheet flexibility but concentrating its production and office activities into fewer locations.

The latest announcement is out from Tompkins Financial Corporation ( (TMP) ).

Tompkins Financial Corporation reported record earnings for the third consecutive quarter, with diluted earnings per share reaching $2.04 in the second quarter of 2026, a 36% increase from a year earlier and 12.1% higher than the first quarter. Net income for the quarter rose to $29.3 million, while first-half 2026 net income climbed 34.6% year over year to $55.4 million, driven by higher net interest income, stronger net interest margin and solid loan growth.

Net interest income grew 23% year over year in the second quarter to $74 million as net interest margin widened to 3.58%, supported by higher yields on earning assets and controlled funding costs, while average loans and deposits both expanded versus 2025. Despite a sharp drop in noninterest income following the sale of its insurance subsidiary in late 2025, Tompkins reduced noninterest expenses and maintained stable asset quality metrics, and its board on July 23, 2026 approved a 13% dividend increase, signaling confidence in the company’s capital strength and earnings momentum.

data.title: KULR Sells Bitcoin Holdings to Eliminate Credit Facility
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

The latest announcement is out from KULR Technology Group ( (KULR) ).

During the period from July 9 to July 23, 2026, KULR Technology Group, Inc. sold approximately 333 bitcoin in open-market transactions at a weighted average price of about $64,538 per BTC, generating roughly $21.5 million in gross proceeds. The sales were part of the company’s treasury management strategy and were completed against a backdrop of heightened market volatility.

KULR used the net proceeds to fully repay its $20 million credit facility with Coinbase Credit, leaving no principal outstanding and positioning the company with a largely debt-free balance sheet. With all 565 BTC previously pledged as collateral expected to be returned and approximately 760 BTC still held as of July 23, 2026, the move is intended to cut interest expense, reduce collateral and liquidation risk, and increase financial flexibility under its newly appointed Board and CFO.

The transaction underscores KULR’s disciplined approach to capital allocation and risk management, balancing continued exposure to bitcoin’s potential upside with a stronger financial foundation. Management framed the decision as a proactive step to prioritize stockholder value while the company continues to scale its core battery technology operations.

Spark’s Take on KULR Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, KULR is a Neutral.

The score is held back most by weak financial performance—large ongoing losses and heavy negative free cash flow—despite solid revenue growth and low current leverage. Offsetting this, the earnings call points to improving commercial traction and a substantial gross-margin step-up with continued cost discipline, though execution risk around the planned H2 production ramp remains high. Technically, the stock shows a mixed picture with longer-term support above key moving averages but muted near-term momentum, while valuation lacks support given negative earnings and no dividend.
